title: [trustless://interop.landscape] Panel - Agentic intents
description:
Josh Rodulf (Ethereum Foundation) moderates a panel with Shaw Walters (Founder of Eliza Labs / Creator of ElizaOS), Davide Crapis (Ethereum Foundation, dAI Team Lead) and Kanishk Khurana (Across Protocol, Senior DevRel Engineer).

As wallets and applications become smarter, the way users interact with blockchains is shifting from manual transactions to higher-level intents. Now, with the emergence of agentic intent architectures, autonomous agents can plan, route, and execute cross-chain actions on behalf of users. This panel brings together leaders working across the field to answer questions like: How do we ensure autonomy without introducing invisible intermediaries or new trust choke points? When a user delegates to an agent, how does the user know what the agent is allowed to do, and not do? How do wallets, bridges, and AI agents coordinate across chains while preserving user control? This panel examines the evolving role of agents in interoperability, intent frameworks, and the new user experience in an agent-dominated world.

Talk given during day 2 of the trustless:// conference at Devconnect Argentina 2025

https://trustlessconference.com
